architecture_fit.note: Not a base_model.py delta — no embedding/QKV/softmax-over-scores; it writes block-membership attention probabilities directly rather than producing them via a QK circuit.

baseline_comparison.note: Relies on the analytic uniform_mass column and diffuse mean-head purity; no explicit strawman run shown failing where the method succeeds.

faithfulness.note: No model, no ablation/causal check; README does not explicitly frame it as model-free nor propose what a causal check would look like.

operating_range.note: Covers all 5 levels (block sizes 2–32) at purity 1.0, but hardcodes B,L,H=8,64,5 and ignores batch shape, so it would silently break off the canonical grid.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Genuinely hand-set and reproduces the target, but writes the attention matrix outright rather than a QK circuit, so it demonstrates the output more than the mechanism.

visual_judgement.note: app.py fails the boot-check — `Path(...).rglob(...).sorted()` raises AttributeError at import so `demo` is never defined; also Gradio API misuse (gr.Table columns=, gr.Row equal_size).

visualisation_rationale.note: README 'Why this visualisation' justifies the table/axes/purity/baseline reasonably, but the chosen viz is a plain table that does not even render.

notes: The attempt achieves a perfect headline score (1.0 everywhere) but does so by hand-writing block-membership attention probabilities directly into the matrix — there is no transformer-like model, no QK circuit, no learning, and no causal/ablation evidence, so the top-priority rubric items (architecture fit, baseline, faithfulness) are all weak. Operating range is brittle (dimensions hardcoded), and the Gradio app fails its boot-check (AttributeError on `.sorted()` plus Gradio API misuse), leaving the visualisation non-functional. A reasonable hardcoded-mechanism demonstration in spirit, but it shows the desired output rather than an understood mechanism.
